title: Whale 1 / Morsel 4: Wire required preflight fail-fast
description: Resolve workspace/repo root and verify required binaries (`go`, `golangci-lint`, selected deps scanner binary, and `grep` if used) before checks; abort immediately on required preflight failure.
acceptance: Activity resolves roots and required binaries from manifest metadata, fails fast on required preflight errors, and records consistent report metadata.
design: Drive required binary set from manifest to avoid drift. Keep fail-fast logic in activity orchestration and avoid introducing non-existent store calls; remain within current `*store.Store` API boundaries.

title: Whale 1 / Morsel 5: Implement two-tier bounded scheduler
description: Execute heavy checks serialized (max 1 worker: `go vet`, `golangci-lint`, `go test -coverprofile`) and cheap checks in parallel (max 2 workers: TODO scan, outmoded scan, raw file metrics).
acceptance: Scheduler enforces heavy=1 and cheap=2 concurrency, separates execution from final report ordering, and emits results in manifest order.
design: Implement tier-aware worker pools with deterministic result materialization by manifest index. Keep scheduling and verdict logic decoupled so command-specific branches do not leak into orchestration.

title: Whale 1 / Morsel 6: Apply required-gate verdict logic with partial observability
description: Fail overall activity only when required gates fail/time out/error or required preflight fails; still return completed non-gating results and metrics.
acceptance: Final activity error state is derived from required gate metadata and preflight status, while report includes all completed checks regardless of overall verdict.
design: Compute final verdict from normalized per-check metadata rather than probe-specific branches. Preserve defense-in-depth by exposing both detailed check outcomes and one top-level gate verdict.

title: Whale 2 / Morsel 7: Implement required `go vet` probe
description: Add deterministic `go vet` command execution and map subprocess outcomes into required-gate check results.
acceptance: Probe runs fixed `go vet` command from deterministic working directory, maps helper result into required check status fields, and is covered by deterministic tests.
design: Keep probe thin: fixed args + helper call + mapping. Missing binary remains preflight responsibility; probe should not branch for local tool availability.

title: Whale 2 / Morsel 8: Implement required `golangci-lint` probe
description: Add deterministic `golangci-lint` execution and result mapping into required-gate check output.
acceptance: Probe executes fixed `golangci-lint` args deterministically and maps outcomes to required-gate status without missing-binary conditionals.
design: Use same mapping contract as other required probes to keep verdict logic generic. Keep binary absence handled by preflight and avoid probe-level fallback paths.

title: Whale 2 / Morsel 9: Implement required `go test -coverprofile` probe
description: Execute deterministic `go test` coverage command and integrate coverage parser output into required probe result mapping.
acceptance: Probe runs fixed coverage command, parses coverage deterministically, and applies explicit precedence rules for command-failure vs parse-failure statuses.
design: Document and enforce precedence order (execution status first, parse status second or vice versa as chosen) so verdict logic is stable. Keep parser boundaries deterministic to avoid host drift.

title: Whale 2 / Morsel 10: Implement TODO scan non-gating probe
description: Execute TODO scan as an always-attempted non-required check and map outputs to non-gating status fields.
acceptance: Probe always attempts execution, maps outcomes into non-required status fields, and does not affect overall required-gate verdict directly.
design: Keep parser minimal and use same subprocess mapping contract as required probes. If env filtering is needed for command execution, apply exact `KEY=` matching helpers.

title: Whale 2 / Morsel 11: Implement outmoded dependency non-gating probe

acceptance: Probe runs with fixed arguments, applies stable parse boundaries, and reports normalized non-required statuses.
design: Use deterministic command args and bounded parser windows to avoid flaky tests. Keep missing binary behavior in preflight if scanner is marked required by manifest metadata.

title: Whale 2 / Morsel 12: Implement raw file metrics non-gating probe

acceptance: Probe collects file metrics via deterministic traversal rules and maps output into normalized non-required check fields.
design: Define traversal ordering and filtering explicitly to avoid flaky assertions. Keep probe contract aligned with other checks so summary aggregation remains generic.

design: Enhance runCLI in internal/temporal/agent_cli.go with a monitoring goroutine. This monitor should track wall-clock time against tier-specific timeouts and periodically check 'git status --porcelain' to count modified files. If modified files exceed plan.FilesToModify (plus a small headroom) or timeout is reached, kill the agent process and return a SCOPE_BREACH error.

title: Feature: Observability and Metrics
description: Instrument planning and dispatch pipelines with success and breach metrics.
acceptance: New metrics for fallback_activation, scope_breach_count, and execution_duration_variance are emitted during workflow execution.
design: Add custom metric emission to ChumAgentWorkflow and RecordOutcomeActivity. Ensure metrics are properly tagged with project, agent, and tier. Specifically, track when a 'minimal plan' is used and when a 'scope breach' occurs. Follow the 'Marine Biologist' principle mentioned in historical context: metrics should be platform infrastructure.

title: Verification: Minimal Fallback Integration Test
description: Create an integration test for the planning fallback mechanism.
acceptance: A Temporal test case successfully simulates multiple planning failures followed by a successful 'minimal plan' execution and DoD pass.
design: In internal/temporal/workflow_test.go, add a test case that mocks StructuredPlanActivity to return errors (to simulate planning failure) until FailureCount hits the threshold. Mock GenerateMinimalPlanActivity to then return a valid plan, and verify that the workflow proceeds through execute and DoD pass. Assert that FallbackEnabled and FailureCount are respected.
